channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Brian Molko Expresses Hope for Yungblud in Placebo Biopic, Notes Accent Challenge
item.description: Brian Molko, the frontman of Placebo, has expressed his desire for Yungblud to portray him in a biopic about the band. In a recent interview with Radio X, Molko praised Yungblud's potential to capture...

item.title: Senato Declines Probe into PFIPC Budget Controversy
item.description: The Senate has turned down a request by Senator Suleiman Kawu (APC, Kano South) to launch an investigation into the operations and controversies surrounding the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promo...

item.title: Argentina stages dramatic comeback against Egypt as Messi scores late equalizer
item.description: In a thrilling World Cup 2026 clash, Argentina staged a remarkable comeback against Egypt to salvage a 2-2 draw in the last-16 tie. Lionel Messi, the 39-year-old maestro, missed a crucial penalty in t...

item.title: Roberto Martinez Steps Down as Portugal Coach Following World Cup Exit
item.description: The Portuguese Football Federation has officially confirmed that Roberto Martinez has resigned as head coach of the national team following their elimination from the FIFA World Cup. The Spaniard, who...

item.title: Footballers Cut Boot Holes to Relieve Heel Pain: Medical Reason Explained
item.description: Portugal winger Pedro Neto was spotted wearing boots with a hole cut near the ankle during his World Cup clash against Spain. The unusual modification is not a fashion statement but a medical solution...

item.title: Energy efficiency gaps between older and newer homes in NSW highlighted by study
item.description: New research reveals significant disparities in energy performance between older and newer homes in New South Wales, with over 850,000 properties rated below 2 stars on the NatHERS scale. Homes built ...

item.title: EPFO Completes National Database Migration for PF Interest Processing by July 15
item.description: The Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has successfully migrated provident fund records from regional databases to a unified national database, enabling members to view their 8.25% interest...

item.title: KP CM Calls for Delay in Tax Exemptions for Merged Districts Amid Merger Concerns
item.description: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi has formally requested Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif to postpone the withdrawal of tax exemptions for merged districts and Malakand Division. The CM emp...

item.title: Globe Readers Share Their Favorite Book Recommendations This Week
item.description: This article highlights weekly book recommendations from Globe and Mail readers, featuring diverse titles that reflect personal reading experiences. Readers share insights on novels like Karen Grose’s...

item.title: Tanzania to Develop 229-Acre Industrial Park in NDC Machame Site
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced plans to develop a 229.13-acre industrial park on a National Development Corporation (NDC) site along the Machame route in Hai District, Kilimanjaro Region. Mini...

item.title: Impeachment Court Adheres to Original Timeline Despite Schedule Adjustments
item.description: The impeachment court in the Philippines is maintaining its adherence to the original timeline for proceedings, despite recent adjustments to the schedule. A spokesperson confirmed that the court rema...

item.title: FIFA Bans English Referees From Officiating Argentina Games Due to Historical Conflict
item.description: The 2026 World Cup has sparked controversy over the ban on English referees officiating matches involving Argentina, a decision rooted in the 1982 Falklands War. English referees Michael Oliver and An...

item.title: ADC Clarifies Aslam Aliyu Wasn't a Member Before Her Defection to APC
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Zamfara State has responded to the defection of Aslam Aliyu to the All Progressives Congress (APC). Aliyu claimed she resigned from ADC due to the party lackin...

item.title: Children's Play Depicts MS Dhoni's Cricket Dreams and Family Responsibilities
item.description: The article describes a children's play titled 'The Incredible Indians: The First Eleven' by Rajesh Talwar, which dramatizes the story of MS Dhoni, the former captain of the Indian men's cricket team....

item.title: New Zealand Startup Tests Fuel-Free Satellite Control Using Superconducting Magnets
item.description: New Zealand startup Zenno Astronautics has successfully tested its 'Supertorquer' superconducting magnet system in orbit, demonstrating a fuel-free method to control satellites using Earth's magnetic ...

item.title: Canadian Soccer Player Shares Disappointing Experience with Frontier Airlines
item.description: Alistair Johnston, a Canadian FIFA World Cup player, shared his frustrating travel experience with Frontier Airlines on social media, criticizing the U.S.-based budget carrier for misrouting him to th...

item.title: South Africa's Clothing Industry Faces Multi-Faceted Challenges
item.description: The clothing industry in KwaZulu-Natal is facing severe challenges from multiple fronts, including government raids, labor law violations, and economic pressures. High-profile raids by authorities hav...

item.title: Serengeti Boys Focus on Learning and Preparation for World Cup
item.description: The Serengeti Boys, representing Tanzania, are prioritizing learning and tactical development over immediate victory in the Four-Nation China International Youth Football Tournament. Their 3-2 win aga...

item.title: Technical access issue for crawler bots on News Corp Australia sites
item.description: The article details a technical message regarding access restrictions for crawler bots on News Corp Australia websites. It explains that automated systems manage bot traffic and outlines steps for use...

item.title: Senator Marcoleta and Mike Defensor Arrested for Receiving Gifts as Public Officials
item.description: The Sandiganbayan 4th Division has ordered the arrest of Senator Rodante Marcoleta and former congressman Mike Defensor for allegedly violating Presidential Decree (PD) No. 46, which prohibits public ...
